name: nvitop
description: An interactive NVIDIA-GPU process viewer and beyond, the one-stop solution for GPU process management.
readme_summary: nvitop is an interactive command-line tool for monitoring and managing NVIDIA GPU processes. It provides real-time insights into GPU utilization, memory usage, and running processes, similar to `htop` for CPUs, and includes features for CUDA visible devices selection and Prometheus exporting.
key_features: Interactive NVIDIA-GPU process viewer
Real-time monitoring of GPU utilization, memory, temperature, power, etc.
Process management (viewing, killing)
CUDA visible devices selection tool
Prometheus exporter for Grafana dashboards
Customizable interface and keybindings
Support for multiple NVIDIA GPUs
primary_use_case: Monitoring and managing NVIDIA GPU processes and resources in real-time from the command line.
cover_image_prompt: A vigilant, futuristic conductor standing before a grand orchestra of glowing NVIDIA GPUs. The conductor holds a baton that directs streams of colorful data, representing processes, to specific GPUs. Each GPU hums with activity, visualized by intricate, glowing circuitry. Small, holographic screens around the conductor display real-time metrics like utilization percentages and memory usage. The overall mood is one of precise control and powerful performance, set against a dark, tech-inspired background with subtle code snippets floating in the air. The style should be a 3D isometric illustration with vibrant, glowing elements and clean lines, emphasizing the 'one-stop solution for GPU process management' aspect.

name: pywalium
description: 🎨 Dynamic Chromium theme generator using Pywal colors
readme_summary: Pywalium is a shell script that generates custom themes for the Chromium browser based on colors defined by the Pywal utility. It automates the process of applying dynamic color schemes to Chromium, allowing users to easily match their browser's appearance to their system's wallpaper or Pywal color palette.
key_features: Generates Chromium themes using Pywal colors
Automates theme creation via a shell script
Easy integration with Chromium's developer mode
Requires re-execution on Pywal color changes
primary_use_case: To dynamically theme the Chromium browser with colors generated by Pywal, ensuring a consistent color scheme across the user's operating system and browser.
cover_image_prompt: A magical alchemist in a dimly lit workshop, pouring a vibrant, liquid rainbow from a Pywal-colored vial into a stylized Chromium browser icon. The liquid flows and reshapes the browser icon, instantly changing its internal UI elements to match the rainbow's colors. Subtle digital particles representing color data swirl around the alchemist's hands and the browser icon. The background features abstract representations of code and color palettes. The scene is rendered in a 3D isometric illustration style, with a focus on glowing, vibrant colors and clear visual transformation, embodying the 'pywalium' name subtly etched on a nearby workbench.

name: TUI_bluetooth
readme_summary: bluetui is a terminal-based TUI manager for Bluetooth devices on Linux. It provides a user-friendly interface to control adapters, manage paired and nearby devices, and interact with audio and TV remotes, all without requiring sudo privileges.
key_features: Device listing with status (connected, paired, nearby), signal strength, and battery level
Adapter control (power, visibility, scanning)
Pairing and connection with PIN/passkey handling
AVRCP media control for audio devices
Automatic audio routing via PulseAudio/PipeWire
Bluetooth HID remote control for Smart TVs
No sudo required, operates via D-Bus
primary_use_case: Managing and interacting with Bluetooth devices from a Linux terminal with a rich TUI.
cover_image_prompt: A friendly, stylized robot hand holding a glowing, intricate remote control, seamlessly interacting with various Bluetooth devices like headphones, a smart TV, and a speaker. The robot hand is connected by subtle, glowing data streams to a clean, minimalist terminal interface displaying device icons and status. The background is a soft, abstract representation of a digital network. The overall mood is one of effortless control and connectivity, emphasizing the TUI's ability to manage complex Bluetooth interactions simply. The style should be a 3D isometric illustration with vibrant, clean colors and clear, readable iconography.

name: VidBee
description: Download videos from almost any website worldwide
readme_summary: VidBee is an open-source video downloader built with Electron and powered by yt-dlp. It allows users to download videos and audio from over 1000 websites worldwide through an intuitive graphical interface. Key features include support for numerous platforms, a user-friendly download queue, and an RSS auto-download function for background updates.
key_features: Download videos and audio from 1000+ websites worldwide
Built with Electron and powered by yt-dlp
Modern, clean, and intuitive user interface
One-click pause/resume/retry functionality
Real-time progress tracking and download queue management
RSS auto-download for automatic updates from subscribed feeds
Configurable output containers
primary_use_case: Downloading videos and audio content from a wide variety of online sources through a desktop application.
cover_image_prompt: A stylized, friendly robot with a large, expressive screen for a face, holding a universal remote control. The robot is standing in front of a giant, overflowing treasure chest filled with digital video icons of various popular streaming services (like YouTube play buttons, TikTok logos, etc.). From the remote, streams of light are 'capturing' these video icons and directing them into a sleek, modern download queue interface that is subtly integrated into the robot's chest. The background is a soft, gradient digital landscape with subtle code-like patterns. The overall mood is helpful and efficient, with a clean, vibrant color palette. The style should be a 3D isometric illustration with smooth, rounded edges and a focus on clear visual storytelling.

name: dexter
description: An autonomous agent for deep financial research
readme_summary: Dexter is an autonomous agent designed for deep financial research. It intelligently plans, executes, and refines research tasks using real-time market data and self-validation to provide confident, data-backed answers to complex financial questions.
key_features: Intelligent Task Planning
Autonomous Execution
Self-Validation
Real-Time Financial Data Access
Safety Features (loop detection, step limits)
primary_use_case: Automating and enhancing deep financial research by providing data-backed answers to complex queries.
cover_image_prompt: A wise, old owl wearing a monocle and a sophisticated vest, perched on a stack of financial reports and glowing data streams. The owl is meticulously examining a complex, holographic financial chart that materializes from a pile of raw data scrolls. Small, subtle UI elements showing code snippets and market tickers float around the owl. The background is a dimly lit, grand library filled with ancient books and modern monitors displaying stock prices. The scene conveys intelligence, deep analysis, and the transformation of raw financial data into clear insights. The style should be a detailed digital painting with a focus on rich textures and a slightly magical, insightful atmosphere, with the repository name 'Dexter' subtly etched on a nearby ledger.
